eurusd.title: Euro weakens as Canadian Dollar receives support from higher oil prices
eurusd.summary: EUR/CAD pares its recent gains from the previous day, trading around 1.6000 during the European hours on Tuesday. The currency cross depreciates as the commodity-linked Canadian Dollar (CAD) holds ground against the Euro (EUR) due to higher oil prices.

eurusd.title: Euro: Retest of 1.1600 against US Dollar seen likely – ING
eurusd.summary: ING’s Francesco Pesole argues that while the European Central Bank (ECB) must stay hawkish to contain long‑end yields, EUR/USD remains vulnerable.

eurusd.title: EURUSD Zigzag Correction Points to Elliott Wave Support at 1.148–1.160
eurusd.summary: EURUSD is unfolding a corrective zigzag structure from the April 17, 2026 high. First, wave A ended at 1.1655, establishing the initial leg of the decline. xauusd.title: Gold Recovers Some Losses: What’s Driving the Market?
xauusd.summary: Gold rose to 4,600 USD per ounce on Tuesday, continuing its recovery from the previous session, and is now trading around 4,548 USD. Market sentiment was supported by hopes of a possible resumption of negotiations between the US and Iran, which has somewhat eased concerns over inflation and the ener

xauusd.title: Gold seems vulnerable near daily low as Fed hike bets and geopolitical risks lift USD
xauusd.summary: Gold (XAU/USD) maintains its offered tone through the early European session on Tuesday and remains well within striking distance of the lowest level since March 30, set the previous day.

xauusd.title: Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD sellers retain control whilst below 21-day SMA near $4,650
xauusd.summary: Gold is back in the red around $4,550 in Asia on Tuesday, having faced rejection once again just shy of the $4,600 mark, as markets digest the latest developments around the conflict between the United States (US) and Iran.

xauusd.title: Gold Eases Amid Middle East Uncertainty
ICYMI - Goldman cuts US recession odds to 25% as Hormuz closure impact stays contained

xauusd.title: Gold price hold steadies, silver rebounds as Hormuz risk keeps oil, yields in focus - PM Report
Ghana seeks 30% of large gold mines’ output for local refineries
xauusd.summary: Africa’s largest bullion producer is seeking an additional 10% on top of the current 20% that large mines must sell to the state-owned Ghana Gold Board.

xauusd.title: Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD buyers are losing the battle
xauusd.summary: XAU/USD bottomed at $4,480 at the beginning of the week, its lowest since late March, as a dismal market sentiment fueled demand for the US Dollar (USD).

xauusd.title: Zambia forms gold venture to formalize mining, build processing
xauusd.summary: The new vehicle will operate in a part of North-Western Province that was the site of a disorderly gold rush that began last year.

xauusd.title: Russia fails to sell stake in gold miner UGC as no one bids at auction
xauusd.summary: The 67.2% stake in UGC - one of Russia's top 10 gold miners - was valued at 140.43 billion roubles.
